title: uniform observational registry (kind, target, phase)
manager: One uniform registry that every hook and observer flows through, keyed by (kind, target, phase) — so instead of a dozen bespoke hook mechanisms there is a single indexed system that construct-hooks, transition-hooks, boundary-hooks, and lifecycle-hooks all register into. This is the architectural spine of the entire hook story: it is what makes hooks introspectable, closeable, and uniform, and it is where the old scattered hook APIs finally converge.
cs: uniform observational registry keyed by (kind, target, phase); every hook kind registers here; enables introspection (W6.78) and closed-enforcement (W6.79); the §12/§14 convergence point that supersedes the on_* grammar cluster.
